CDH1 14.09 14.54 14.13 6.95e-04 0.385 0.385 
library(GSVA)
sf <- "results/rds/hybrid_ssgsea.rds"
if (!file.exists(sf)) {
  sigs <- list(basal=basal_ids, luminal=lum_ids)
  par <- ssgseaParam(exprData=as.matrix(log2(norm_counts+1)), geneSets=sigs)
  ss <- gsva(par, verbose=FALSE); saveRDS(ss, sf)
} else ss <- readRDS(sf)
sdf <- data.frame(barcode=colnames(ss), basal=ss["basal",], luminal=ss["luminal",])
sdf <- merge(sdf, group_df[,c("barcode","group")], by="barcode")
fig_ssgsea <- ggplot(sdf, aes(luminal, basal, color=group)) + geom_point(alpha=0.5, size=1.5) +
  stat_ellipse(level=0.68, linewidth=1) + scale_color_manual(values=grp_colors) +
  labs(title="2D lineage identity space (centaur: basal up, luminal flat)",
       x="Luminal ssGSEA", y="Basal ssGSEA") + theme_bw(base_size=12)
fig_ssgsea

7.2 Proliferative Quiescence (Luminal-Associated)

prolif_ids <- get_ids(proliferation)
pscore <- colMeans(log2(norm_counts[prolif_ids, group_df$barcode]+1), na.rm=TRUE)
pdf <- merge(data.frame(barcode=names(pscore), prolif=pscore),
  group_df[,c("barcode","group")], by="barcode")
pcor <- data.frame()
for (sym in names(prolif_ids)) {
  ct <- cor.test(foxc1_lumA, as.numeric(norm_counts[prolif_ids[sym], lumA_idx]),
                  method="spearman", exact=FALSE)
  pcor <- rbind(pcor, data.frame(gene=sym, rho=round(ct$estimate,3)))
}
fig_prolif_box <- ggplot(pdf, aes(group, prolif, fill=group)) +
  geom_violin(alpha=0.6) + geom_boxplot(width=0.15, outlier.size=0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=grp_colors) +
  labs(title="Proliferation by group", subtitle="Centaur ≈ non-centaur << LumB < Basal",
       x=NULL, y="Mean log2 prolif") + theme_bw(base_size=12) + theme(legend.position="none")
fig_prolif_cor <- ggplot(pcor, aes(rho, reorder(gene, rho))) + geom_col(fill="#7f8c8d") +
  geom_vline(xintercept=c(-0.3,0,0.3), linetype=c("dashed","solid","dashed")) +
  labs(title=paste("Mean ρ =", round(mean(pcor$rho),3)), x="ρ with FOXC1", y=NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size=10)
fig_prolif_box | fig_prolif_cor

7.3 Progenitor Markers

# LP_PANEL (6 genes) is defined globally and is the COMPOSITE used in mediation (prog_z).
# Do NOT add genes to LP_PANEL here -- doing so would change the mediation HR in s7.
# For reporting we ALSO correlate the negative markers CD24/EPCAM/CD44 -- correlation
# only, NOT folded into the composite.
prog_pos <- LP_PANEL                      # positive progenitor markers (validated panel)
prog_neg <- c("CD24","EPCAM","CD44")      # negative markers, correlation only

cor_one <- function(sym) {
  gid <- lookup_id(sym); if (is.na(gid)) return(NULL)
  ct <- cor.test(foxc1_lumA, as.numeric(norm_counts[gid, lumA_idx]),
                  method="spearman", exact=FALSE)
  data.frame(gene=sym, rho=round(ct$estimate,3), p=signif(ct$p.value,3))
}
prog <- do.call(rbind, lapply(c(prog_pos, prog_neg), cor_one))
prog$direction <- ifelse(prog$gene %in% prog_neg, "negative", "positive")
prog$fdr <- signif(p.adjust(prog$p, "BH"), 3)
prog <- prog[order(-prog$rho), ]

# Output the table
knitr::kable(prog, caption="FOXC1 × progenitor markers in LumA (BH-corrected; negative markers are reported but NOT in the mediation composite)")
FOXC1 × progenitor markers in LumA (BH-corrected; negative markers are reported but NOT in the mediation composite)
gene rho p direction fdr
rho KIT 0.584 0.00000 positive 0.0000
rho1 ALDH1A3 0.460 0.00000 positive 0.0000
rho2 PROM1 0.376 0.00000 positive 0.0000
rho5 ELF5 0.361 0.00000 positive 0.0000
rho4 SLPI 0.344 0.00000 positive 0.0000
rho3 KRT15 0.277 0.00000 positive 0.0000
rho8 CD44 -0.096 0.02180 negative 0.0218
rho6 CD24 -0.102 0.01510 negative 0.0170
rho7 EPCAM -0.108 0.00976 negative 0.0125 
# Then the plot
fig_prog <- ggplot(prog, aes(rho, reorder(gene, rho), fill=direction)) +
  geom_col() + geom_vline(xintercept=0) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c(positive="#2c3e50", negative="#c0392b")) +
  labs(title="Progenitor markers vs FOXC1 in LumA", x="Spearman ρ", y=NULL, fill=NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size=12)
fig_prog

7.4 Paracrine Factors by Cellular Source

myo_para  <- c("FGF2","HGF","NRG1")
strom_para<- c("BMP2","BMP4","FGF7","PDGFA","PDGFB")
mixed_para<- c("IGF1","TGFB1","TGFB2","TGFB3","WNT5A")
para_all <- c(myo_para, strom_para, mixed_para)
para_ids <- get_ids(para_all)
para <- data.frame()
for (sym in names(para_ids)) {
  ct <- cor.test(foxc1_lumA, as.numeric(norm_counts[para_ids[sym], lumA_idx]),
                  method="spearman", exact=FALSE)
  vals <- log2(as.numeric(norm_counts[para_ids[sym], group_df$barcode])+1)
  meds <- tapply(vals, group_df$group, median, na.rm=TRUE)
  src <- case_when(sym %in% myo_para~"Myoepithelial", sym %in% strom_para~"Stromal",
                    TRUE~"Mixed/uncertain")
  para <- rbind(para, data.frame(gene=sym, source=src, rho=round(ct$estimate,3),
    med_centaur=round(meds["Centaur"],2), med_basal=round(meds["Basal"],2)))
}
para <- para[order(-para$rho), ]
fig_para <- ggplot(para, aes(rho, reorder(gene, rho), fill=source)) + geom_col() + geom_vline(xintercept=0) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c(Myoepithelial="#e74c3c", Stromal="#3498db",
    `Mixed/uncertain`="#95a5a6")) +
  labs(title="Paracrine factors by normal cellular source",
       subtitle="Myoepithelial (FGF2/NRG1/HGF) exceed Basal in centaur = cancer-intrinsic",
       x="Spearman ρ with FOXC1", y=NULL, fill="Normal source") + theme_bw(base_size=11)
fig_para

knitr::kable(para, caption="Paracrine: correlation + median expression vs Basal")
Paracrine: correlation + median expression vs Basal
gene source rho med_centaur med_basal
rho3 BMP2 Stromal 0.640 6.63 7.36
rho8 IGF1 Mixed/uncertain 0.577 7.66 5.52
rho5 FGF7 Stromal 0.533 9.32 8.47
rho FGF2 Myoepithelial 0.527 8.78 8.60
rho6 PDGFA Stromal 0.440 10.05 9.75
rho2 NRG1 Myoepithelial 0.436 8.19 7.05
rho1 HGF Myoepithelial 0.386 8.76 7.13
rho11 TGFB3 Mixed/uncertain 0.326 12.44 10.26
rho9 TGFB1 Mixed/uncertain 0.254 11.80 11.21
rho10 TGFB2 Mixed/uncertain 0.235 9.53 9.76
rho12 WNT5A Mixed/uncertain 0.181 10.80 9.56
rho4 BMP4 Stromal 0.180 9.43 6.94
rho7 PDGFB Stromal 0.158 11.28 10.82

7.5 Three-Tier Inflammatory Regulation

inflam <- c("IL6","CXCL8","CXCL1","CXCL2","CXCL3","CCL2","CCL7","PTGS2","IL1A","IL1B")
inf_ids <- get_ids(inflam)
inf <- data.frame()
for (sym in names(inf_ids)) {
  vals <- log2(as.numeric(norm_counts[inf_ids[sym], group_df$barcode])+1)
  meds <- tapply(vals, group_df$group, median, na.rm=TRUE)
  inf <- rbind(inf, data.frame(gene=sym,
    delta_vs_basal=round(meds["Centaur"]-meds["Basal"],2)))
}
inf$tier <- ifelse(inf$delta_vs_basal > -0.3, "Tier1: Autonomous",
  ifelse(inf$delta_vs_basal > -1.0, "Tier2: FOXC1-restrained", "Tier3: TAM-dependent"))
inf <- inf[order(inf$delta_vs_basal), ]
fig_inflam <- ggplot(inf, aes(delta_vs_basal, reorder(gene, delta_vs_basal), fill=tier)) +
  geom_col() + geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="dashed") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c(`Tier1: Autonomous`="#27ae60",
    `Tier2: FOXC1-restrained`="#f39c12", `Tier3: TAM-dependent`="#e74c3c")) +
  labs(title="Inflammatory genes: Centaur vs Basal", x="Δ median log2", y=NULL, fill=NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size=11)
fig_inflam

8 Mediation & Independence

8.1 Clinical Sequential Cox

surv_cc <- surv_norad[complete.cases(surv_norad[,c("group","age","stage_clean")]), ]
m1 <- coxph(Surv(time,event)~group, data=surv_norad)
m2 <- coxph(Surv(time,event)~group+age, data=surv_cc)
m3 <- coxph(Surv(time,event)~group+stage_clean, data=surv_cc)
m4 <- coxph(Surv(time,event)~group+age+stage_clean, data=surv_cc)
m5 <- coxph(Surv(time,event)~group+age+stage_clean+cyto_z, data=surv_cc)
rbind(
  cbind(Model="Centaur only (full)", extract_hr(m1)),
  cbind(Model="+ age", extract_hr(m2)), cbind(Model="+ stage", extract_hr(m3)),
  cbind(Model="+ age + stage", extract_hr(m4)),
  cbind(Model="+ age + stage + cytotoxic", extract_hr(m5))
) %>% knitr::kable(caption="Clinical covariate adjustment")
Clinical covariate adjustment
Model HR Lower Upper p
Centaur only (full) 0.282 0.094 0.844 0.0237
+ age 0.386 0.124 1.204 0.1010
+ stage 0.333 0.107 1.039 0.0582
+ age + stage 0.422 0.132 1.345 0.1450
+ age + stage + cytotoxic 0.737 0.199 2.734 0.6490

8.2 Causal Chain (Individual Mediators, age+stage controlled)

surv_cc$prog_z  <- make_score(LP_PANEL, surv_cc$barcode)
surv_cc$strom_z <- make_score(c("PECAM1","CDH5","PDGFRA","ACTA2","ACKR1"), surv_cc$barcode)
surv_cc$tls_z   <- make_score(c("CCL19","CCL21","CXCL13"), surv_cc$barcode)
surv_cc$cyto_z  <- make_score(c("CD8A","CD8B","GZMA","GZMB","PRF1","GNLY","NKG7","IFNG"), surv_cc$barcode)
rbind(
  cbind(Model="age+stage baseline", extract_hr(coxph(Surv(time,event)~group+age+stage_clean, data=surv_cc))),
  cbind(Model="+ progenitor", extract_hr(coxph(Surv(time,event)~group+age+stage_clean+prog_z, data=surv_cc))),
  cbind(Model="+ stromal", extract_hr(coxph(Surv(time,event)~group+age+stage_clean+strom_z, data=surv_cc))),
  cbind(Model="+ TLS", extract_hr(coxph(Surv(time,event)~group+age+stage_clean+tls_z, data=surv_cc))),
  cbind(Model="+ cytotoxic", extract_hr(coxph(Surv(time,event)~group+age+stage_clean+cyto_z, data=surv_cc)))
) %>% knitr::kable(caption="Individual mediator adjustment (each vs age+stage baseline)")
Individual mediator adjustment (each vs age+stage baseline)
Model HR Lower Upper p
age+stage baseline 0.422 0.132 1.345 0.145
+ progenitor 0.636 0.175 2.315 0.492
+ stromal 0.514 0.128 2.069 0.349
+ TLS 0.671 0.171 2.628 0.567
+ cytotoxic 0.737 0.199 2.734 0.649

8.3 Age corelations with progenitors, foxc1

# Direct correlation of FOXC1 and progenitor markers WITH age
age_genes <- c("FOXC1", "KIT", "ALDH1A3", "PROM1", "KRT15", "SLPI", "ELF5")
age_lumA <- as.numeric(sample_meta_tumor$age_at_diagnosis[lumA_idx])/365.25
stage_num <- as.numeric(factor(gsub("[ABC]$","",gsub("Stage ","",
  sample_meta_tumor$ajcc_pathologic_stage[lumA_idx])), levels=c("I","II","III","IV")))

vb <- !is.na(age_lumA) & !is.na(stage_num)

age_cor <- data.frame()
for (sym in age_genes) {
  gid <- lookup_id(sym)
  if (is.na(gid)) next
  expr <- as.numeric(norm_counts[gid, lumA_idx])
  ct <- cor.test(expr[vb], age_lumA[vb], method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  age_cor <- rbind(age_cor, data.frame(
    gene = sym, rho_vs_age = round(ct$estimate, 3),
    p = signif(ct$p.value, 3)))
}
age_cor$fdr <- signif(p.adjust(age_cor$p, "BH"), 3)
knitr::kable(age_cor,
  caption = "FOXC1 and progenitor markers vs patient age (inverse = younger patients enriched)")
FOXC1 and progenitor markers vs patient age (inverse = younger patients enriched)
gene rho_vs_age p fdr
rho FOXC1 -0.170 1.35e-04 2.36e-04
rho1 KIT -0.222 5.00e-07 3.70e-06
rho2 ALDH1A3 -0.200 6.50e-06 1.52e-05
rho3 PROM1 -0.202 5.40e-06 1.52e-05
rho4 KRT15 -0.012 7.90e-01 7.90e-01
rho5 SLPI 0.040 3.69e-01 4.30e-01
rho6 ELF5 -0.080 7.40e-02 1.04e-01 
# Optional: visualize FOXC1 vs age directly
fig_foxc1_age <- ggplot(data.frame(foxc1 = foxc1_lumA[vb], age = age_lumA[vb]),
       aes(age, log2(foxc1 + 1))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#984ea3") +
  labs(title = "FOXC1 declines with patient age in LumA",
       subtitle = paste0("Spearman ρ = ",
         round(cor(foxc1_lumA[vb], age_lumA[vb], method = "spearman"), 3)),
       x = "Age at diagnosis (years)", y = "FOXC1 log2") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)
fig_foxc1_age

8.4 Age Confound & Partial Correlations

age_lumA <- as.numeric(sample_meta_tumor$age_at_diagnosis[lumA_idx])/365.25
stage_num <- as.numeric(factor(gsub("[ABC]$","",gsub("Stage ","",
  sample_meta_tumor$ajcc_pathologic_stage[lumA_idx])), levels=c("I","II","III","IV")))
vb <- !is.na(age_lumA) & !is.na(stage_num)
psp <- function(x,y,c1,c2){ ok<-complete.cases(x,y,c1,c2)
  cor.test(residuals(lm(rank(x[ok])~rank(c1[ok])+rank(c2[ok]))),
           residuals(lm(rank(y[ok])~rank(c1[ok])+rank(c2[ok])))) }
kg <- c("CD8A","PRF1","CCL19","CCL21","CD79B","PDCD1",
        "ACKR1","PECAM1","PDGFRA","CXCL12",
        "FGF2","NRG1","HGF","BMP2","IGF1",
        "KIT","ALDH1A3","PROM1",
        "CD68","CXCL1")
adj <- data.frame()
for (sym in kg) { gid<-lookup_id(sym); if(is.na(gid)) next
  tg <- as.numeric(norm_counts[gid, lumA_idx])
  raw <- cor.test(foxc1_lumA[vb], tg[vb], method="spearman", exact=FALSE)
  pc <- psp(foxc1_lumA, tg, age_lumA, stage_num)
  adj <- rbind(adj, data.frame(gene=sym, raw=round(raw$estimate,3),
    adj=round(pc$estimate,3), p=signif(pc$p.value,3))) }
adj$sig <- ifelse(p.adjust(adj$p,"bonferroni")<0.05,"Sig","NS")
fig_age_partial <- ggplot(adj, aes(raw, adj, color=sig, label=gene)) + geom_point(size=3) +
  geom_text(hjust=-0.15, size=3, show.legend=FALSE) +
  geom_abline(slope=1, intercept=0, linetype="dashed", color="grey50") +
  scale_color_manual(values=c(Sig="#2c3e50", NS="#e74c3c")) +
  labs(title="Age+stage partial correlations (above diagonal = suppressor)",
       x="Raw ρ", y="Adjusted ρ") + theme_bw(base_size=12)
fig_age_partial

8.5 Age-Stratified Survival

surv_norad$strat <- cut(surv_norad$age, c(0,50,65,100), c("<50","50-65",">65"))
as_res <- data.frame()
for (s in c("<50","50-65",">65")) {
  sub <- surv_norad[surv_norad$strat==s & !is.na(surv_norad$strat), ]
  if (sum(sub$event)>=3 && length(unique(sub$group))==2) {
    sc <- summary(coxph(Surv(time,event)~group, data=sub))
    as_res <- rbind(as_res, data.frame(Age=s, N=nrow(sub), Events=sum(sub$event),
      HR=round(sc$conf.int[1,1],2), p=signif(sc$coefficients[1,5],3)))
  }
}
knitr::kable(as_res, caption="Age-stratified survival (HR stable across strata)")
Age-stratified survival (HR stable across strata)
Age N Events HR p
50-65 80 6 0.34 0.349
>65 85 13 0.36 0.118

9 Orthogonality to Existing Classifications

foxc1_grp <- ifelse(foxc1_lumA >= foxc1_med, "Centaur", "Non-centaur")
score_of <- function(genes) colMeans(log2(norm_counts[get_ids(genes), lumA_idx]+1), na.rm=TRUE)

# Clinical tools (proliferation-dominated)
odx   <- scale(score_of(c("MKI67","AURKA","BIRC5","CCNB1","MYBL2")))[,1] -
         0.5*scale(score_of(c("ESR1","PGR","BCL2","SCUBE2")))[,1]
mamma <- score_of(c("CCNE2","MCM6","CENPA","MMP9","CXCL12","FLT1","IGFBP5","SCUBE2"))
ror   <- score_of(c("BIRC5","CCNB1","CDC20","MKI67","RRM2","TYMS","UBE2C","MYBL2"))
aurka <- as.numeric(norm_counts[lookup_id("AURKA"), lumA_idx])
# Poudel signatures
poudel_stem <- score_of(c("LGR5","ASCL2","AXIN2","SOX9","CD44","PROM1"))
poudel_inf  <- score_of(c("CD2","CD3D","CD8A","GZMB","CXCL9","CXCL10"))
poudel_mes  <- score_of(c("ZEB1","ZEB2","VIM","FN1","SPARC","COL1A1"))
# [REVIEW FIX] Lineage contrast (luminal - basal). This is a LINEAGE-IDENTITY axis, NOT tumor
# purity: it contains FOXC1, so its correlation with the Centaur axis is circular ("by definition").
# It must NOT be used as a purity/admixture control (it was previously mislabeled "purity").
lineage_contrast <- score_of(c("ESR1","PGR","FOXA1","GATA3")) -
                    score_of(c("KRT5","KRT14","KRT17","EGFR","FOXC1"))

# REAL tumor purity for the admixture confound check -- a DNA/consensus estimate INDEPENDENT of the
# lineage RNA. Priority: (1) cached rds; (2) Aran 2015 consensus purity bundled in TCGAbiolinks
# (CPE = consensus of ESTIMATE/ABSOLUTE/LUMP/IHC; ABSOLUTE = DNA copy-number), filling CPE gaps with
# ABSOLUTE; (3) NA -> dependent checks skip. The whole thing is tryCatch-wrapped so a missing package
# or a failed barcode match can NEVER halt the knit. To use a DNA-only estimate, swap CPE for ABSOLUTE.
purity_file <- "results/rds/tcga_brca_purity_cpe.rds"
.lumA_bc <- sample_meta_tumor$barcode[lumA_idx]
purity <- tryCatch({
  if (file.exists(purity_file)) {
    pv <- readRDS(purity_file)
    as.numeric(pv[ match(substr(.lumA_bc, 1, 15), substr(names(pv), 1, 15)) ])
  } else if (requireNamespace("TCGAbiolinks", quietly = TRUE)) {
    .e <- new.env(); utils::data("Tumor.purity", package = "TCGAbiolinks", envir = .e)
    tp <- .e$Tumor.purity
    num  <- function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", as.character(x))))
    cpe  <- if ("CPE" %in% names(tp))      num(tp$CPE)      else rep(NA_real_, nrow(tp))
    abs_ <- if ("ABSOLUTE" %in% names(tp)) num(tp$ABSOLUTE) else rep(NA_real_, nrow(tp))
    cpe[is.na(cpe)] <- abs_[is.na(cpe)]                       # fill CPE gaps with ABSOLUTE
    idcol <- if ("Sample.ID" %in% names(tp)) "Sample.ID" else names(tp)[1]
    pv <- stats::setNames(cpe, as.character(tp[[idcol]]))
    # Match LumA barcodes at the substring length that resolves the most samples.
    best_k <- 15; best_n <- -1
    for (k in c(16, 15, 12)) {
      n <- length(intersect(substr(.lumA_bc, 1, k), substr(names(pv), 1, k)))
      if (n > best_n) { best_n <- n; best_k <- k }
    }
    out <- as.numeric(pv[ match(substr(.lumA_bc, 1, best_k), substr(names(pv), 1, best_k)) ])
    message(sprintf("Purity: TCGAbiolinks consensus (CPE/ABSOLUTE) matched %d/%d LumA at %d-char.",
                    sum(!is.na(out)), length(.lumA_bc), best_k))
    try({ dir.create(dirname(purity_file), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
          saveRDS(stats::setNames(out, .lumA_bc), purity_file) }, silent = TRUE)  # cache (best-effort)
    out
  } else {
    message("Purity: TCGAbiolinks not installed and no cached file -- admixture checks will skip.")
    rep(NA_real_, sum(lumA_idx))
  }
}, error = function(e) {
  message("Purity load failed (", conditionMessage(e), ") -- admixture checks will skip.")
  rep(NA_real_, sum(lumA_idx))
})
cat(sprintf("Real purity available for %d / %d LumA tumors.\n", sum(!is.na(purity)), length(purity)))
## Real purity available for 564 / 571 LumA tumors.
ortho <- data.frame(
  System=c("Oncotype DX","MammaPrint","PAM50 ROR","SCMGENE AURKA",
           "Poudel stem","Poudel inflammatory","Poudel mesenchymal","Lineage contrast (lum-basal)"),
  rho=c(
    cor(foxc1_lumA, odx, method="spearman"),
    cor(foxc1_lumA, mamma, method="spearman"),
    cor(foxc1_lumA, ror, method="spearman"),
    cor(foxc1_lumA, aurka, method="spearman"),
    cor(foxc1_lumA, poudel_stem, method="spearman"),
    cor(foxc1_lumA, poudel_inf, method="spearman"),
    cor(foxc1_lumA, poudel_mes, method="spearman"),
    cor(foxc1_lumA, lineage_contrast, method="spearman")))
ortho$rho <- round(ortho$rho, 3)
ortho$interpretation <- c(rep("Clinical tool (expect null)",4),
  rep("Poudel (expect overlap)",3), "By definition")
knitr::kable(ortho, caption="FOXC1 orthogonality to LumA classification systems")
FOXC1 orthogonality to LumA classification systems
System rho interpretation
Oncotype DX 0.038 Clinical tool (expect null)
MammaPrint 0.061 Clinical tool (expect null)
PAM50 ROR -0.086 Clinical tool (expect null)
SCMGENE AURKA -0.128 Clinical tool (expect null)
Poudel stem 0.420 Poudel (expect overlap)
Poudel inflammatory 0.352 Poudel (expect overlap)
Poudel mesenchymal 0.331 Poudel (expect overlap)
Lineage contrast (lum-basal) -0.701 By definition 
# Single-gene lineage anchors cited in the text (SCMGENE genes + ER axis).
# Reproduces ESR1 rho (~ -0.43) and confirms ERBB2 is uninformative in LumA.
anchor <- data.frame()
for (sym in c("ESR1","PGR","ERBB2")) {
  gid <- lookup_id(sym); if (is.na(gid)) next
  ct <- cor.test(foxc1_lumA, as.numeric(norm_counts[gid, lumA_idx]),
                 method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  anchor <- rbind(anchor, data.frame(gene = sym,
    rho = round(ct$estimate, 3), p = signif(ct$p.value, 3)))
}
knitr::kable(anchor, row.names = FALSE,
  caption = "FOXC1 vs single-gene lineage anchors in LumA (ESR1 is the cited -0.43)")
FOXC1 vs single-gene lineage anchors in LumA (ESR1 is the cited -0.43)
gene rho p
ESR1 -0.430 0.00e+00
PGR -0.168 5.75e-05
ERBB2 -0.065 1.18e-01 
fig_ortho <- ggplot(ortho, aes(reorder(System, abs(rho)), abs(rho), fill=abs(rho)>0.3)) +
  geom_col() + coord_flip() +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0.3, linetype="dashed", color="red") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c(`TRUE`="#e74c3c", `FALSE`="#2c3e50"), guide="none") +
  labs(title="Orthogonality: clinical tools blind to centaur",
       subtitle="Red line = substantial overlap (0.3)", x=NULL, y="|Spearman ρ|") +
  theme_bw(base_size=12)
fig_ortho

# [REVIEW FIX] The (luminal - basal) score below is a LINEAGE-IDENTITY contrast, NOT tumor purity
# (it contains FOXC1). Its association with the Centaur axis is circular, so it CANNOT serve as an
# admixture control. We (a) keep it only as a labeled lineage axis, and (b) run the real admixture
# check against a TRUE purity estimate ('purity', loaded in setup) if one was supplied.
lineage_contrast_v <- (score_of(c("ESR1","PGR","FOXA1","GATA3")) -
                       score_of(c("KRT5","KRT14","KRT17","EGFR","FOXC1")))
names(lineage_contrast_v) <- sample_meta_tumor$barcode[lumA_idx]
surv_pur <- surv_norad
surv_pur$lin_z <- scale(lineage_contrast_v[surv_norad$barcode])[,1]
spL <- surv_pur[!is.na(surv_pur$lin_z), ]
cat("[lineage contrast, NOT purity] alone HR:",
    round(summary(coxph(Surv(time,event)~lin_z, data=spL))$conf.int[1,1],3),
    "(circular with the Centaur definition; reported only as a lineage axis)\n")
## [lineage contrast, NOT purity] alone HR: 1.657 (circular with the Centaur definition; reported only as a lineage axis)
# --- REAL purity sensitivity (skips if no DNA/CPE purity supplied) ---
if (exists("purity") && any(!is.na(purity))) {
  pr <- purity; names(pr) <- sample_meta_tumor$barcode[lumA_idx]
  surv_pur$pur_z <- scale(pr[surv_norad$barcode])[,1]
  spR <- surv_pur[!is.na(surv_pur$pur_z), ]
  cat("[REAL purity] alone HR:", round(summary(coxph(Surv(time,event)~pur_z, data=spR))$conf.int[1,1],3), "\n")
  cat("[REAL purity] Centaur | purity HR:",
      round(summary(coxph(Surv(time,event)~pur_z+group, data=spR))$conf.int[cn,1],3),
      "(does the Centaur benefit survive a TRUE purity adjustment?)\n")
} else {
  cat("[REAL purity check skipped] supply results/rds/tcga_brca_purity_cpe.rds (ABSOLUTE/CPE) to enable.\n")
}
## [REAL purity] alone HR: 1.448 
## [REAL purity] Centaur | purity HR: 0.312 (does the Centaur benefit survive a TRUE purity adjustment?)

#comparison between poudel subtypes and centaur phenotype

# ── Load Poudel's actual per-sample heterocellular calls ──
poudel <- read.csv("poudel_heterocellular_calls.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Poudel uses dot-separated 12-char patient IDs (TCGA.A2.A0CO).
# Harmonize to dash format to match your barcodes.
poudel$patient12 <- gsub("\\.", "-", poudel$Sample)

# Your LumA tumors → 12-char patient ID
group_df$patient12 <- substr(group_df$patient, 1, 12)

# ── Join (LumA only) ──
merged <- merge(
  group_df[group_df$pam50 == "LumA", c("patient12", "group")],
  poudel[, c("patient12", "Heterocellular_Subtype")],
  by = "patient12")

cat("Matched LumA tumors:", nrow(merged), "\n")
## Matched LumA tumors: 419
cat("Subtypes present:\n"); print(table(merged$Heterocellular_Subtype))
## Subtypes present:
## 
##           Enterocyte          Goblet.like         Inflammatory 
##                   33                   34                   25 
##  Low confidence only           Mixed only Mixed/Low confidence 
##                   36                   74                  108 
##            Stem.like                   TA 
##                   87                   22
# ── Restrict to high-confidence calls (drop mixed/low) ──
hc <- c("Enterocyte","Goblet.like","Inflammatory","Stem.like","TA")
mh <- merged[merged$Heterocellular_Subtype %in% hc, ]
cat("\nHigh-confidence LumA tumors:", nrow(mh), "\n")
## 
## High-confidence LumA tumors: 201
# ── The decisive cross-tab ──
tab <- table(mh$Heterocellular_Subtype,
             ifelse(mh$group == "Centaur", "Centaur", "Non_centaur"))
cat("\nCentaur vs Poudel subtype:\n"); print(tab)
## 
## Centaur vs Poudel subtype:
##               
##                Centaur Non_centaur
##   Enterocyte        26           7
##   Goblet.like       10          24
##   Inflammatory      14          11
##   Stem.like         46          41
##   TA                 2          20
cat("\n% centaur within each subtype (baseline ~50%):\n")
## 
## % centaur within each subtype (baseline ~50%):
print(round(prop.table(tab, 1), 2))
##               
##                Centaur Non_centaur
##   Enterocyte      0.79        0.21
##   Goblet.like     0.29        0.71
##   Inflammatory    0.56        0.44
##   Stem.like       0.53        0.47
##   TA              0.09        0.91
# ── The numbers that settle it ──
cat("\n% of centaur tumors that are Poudel Stem.like:",
    round(100*mean(mh$Heterocellular_Subtype[mh$group=="Centaur"]=="Stem.like"),1),"%\n")
## 
## % of centaur tumors that are Poudel Stem.like: 46.9 %
cat("% of Poudel Stem.like that are centaur:",
    round(100*mean(mh$group[mh$Heterocellular_Subtype=="Stem.like"]=="Centaur"),1),"%\n")
## % of Poudel Stem.like that are centaur: 52.9 %
chi <- chisq.test(table(mh$Heterocellular_Subtype, mh$group))
g2 <- ifelse(mh$group == "Centaur", "Centaur", "Non")
tab2 <- table(mh$Heterocellular_Subtype, g2)
chi <- chisq.test(tab2)
V <- sqrt(as.numeric(chi$statistic) / (nrow(mh) * (min(dim(tab2)) - 1)))
cat("Cramer's V:", round(V, 3), "| chi-sq p =", signif(chi$p.value, 3), "\n")
## Cramer's V: 0.399 | chi-sq p = 1.94e-06

#orthogonality to clinical genomic risk tools

# === s8_clinical_tools: FOXC1 vs clinical genomic risk scores ===
library(genefu)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)

# genefu expects samples x genes; build expression + annotation
lumA_expr <- t(log2(norm_counts[, lumA_idx] + 1))           # samples x genes
ens <- gsub("\\..*", "", colnames(lumA_expr))               # strip version
annot <- data.frame(
  probe         = colnames(lumA_expr),
  Gene.Symbol   = mapIds(org.Hs.eg.db, ens, "SYMBOL",  "ENSEMBL"),
  EntrezGene.ID = mapIds(org.Hs.eg.db, ens, "ENTREZID","ENSEMBL"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# FOXC1 in the same sample order as lumA_expr rows
foxc1 <- as.numeric(foxc1_lumA)   # built from norm_counts[foxc1_id, lumA_idx]
stopifnot(length(foxc1) == nrow(lumA_expr))

# Compute each validated risk score
odx <- oncotypedx(data = lumA_expr, annot = annot, do.mapping = TRUE)$score
mp  <- gene70   (data = lumA_expr, annot = annot, do.mapping = TRUE)$score
ror <- rorS     (data = lumA_expr, annot = annot, do.mapping = TRUE)$score
# SCMGENE proliferation axis = AURKA expression (ESR1/ERBB2/AURKA model)
aurka <- as.numeric(lumA_expr[, annot$probe[which(annot$Gene.Symbol == "AURKA")[1]]])

tools <- list("Oncotype DX" = odx, "MammaPrint" = mp,
              "PAM50 ROR" = ror, "SCMGENE AURKA" = aurka)

cat("FOXC1 correlation with clinical genomic risk tools (LumA):\n")
## FOXC1 correlation with clinical genomic risk tools (LumA):
for (nm in names(tools)) {
  ct <- cor.test(foxc1, tools[[nm]], method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  cat(sprintf("  %-16s rho=%+.3f  p=%.2e\n", nm, ct$estimate, ct$p.value))
}
##   Oncotype DX      rho=+0.036  p=3.92e-01
##   MammaPrint       rho=-0.015  p=7.16e-01
##   PAM50 ROR        rho=-0.529  p=1.79e-42
##   SCMGENE AURKA    rho=-0.128  p=2.24e-03
# ROR-S (subtype-correlation only) — exact, from genefu
ror_s <- rorS(data = lumA_expr, annot = annot, do.mapping = TRUE)$score

# PAM50 11-gene proliferation signature (Nielsen 2010 / Parker 2009)
prolif_genes <- c("BIRC5","CCNB1","CDC20","CEP55","MKI67","NDC80",
                  "NUF2","PTTG1","RRM2","TYMS","UBE2C")
pg <- annot$probe[annot$Gene.Symbol %in% prolif_genes]
prolif <- rowMeans(scale(lumA_expr[, pg]))

# ROR-P = subtype component + proliferation component
# Parker 2009 combines them with model-fit weights; z-scored sum below is a
# transparent stand-in. For the manuscript, use Parker's published proliferation
# coefficient so a reviewer reproduces your exact value.
ror_p <- as.numeric(scale(ror_s)) + as.numeric(scale(prolif))

ct <- function(x){ r <- cor.test(foxc1, x, method="spearman", exact=FALSE)
                   sprintf("rho=%+.3f  p=%.2e", r$estimate, r$p.value) }
cat("ROR-S (subtype only):    ", ct(ror_s),  "\n")
## ROR-S (subtype only):     rho=-0.529  p=1.79e-42
cat("Proliferation (PAM50):   ", ct(prolif), "\n")
## Proliferation (PAM50):    rho=-0.094  p=2.40e-02
cat("ROR-P (subtype+prolif):  ", ct(ror_p),  "\n")
## ROR-P (subtype+prolif):   rho=-0.354  p=2.75e-18

#centaur symptoms unexpalinable by tumor purity

# ============================================================
# Section 8.4 — Centaur is not explained by tumor purity / admixture
# ============================================================
library(genefu); library(survival); library(dplyr)
data(pam50.robust)   # load the PAM50 centroid model (not auto-loaded by library())

# ============================================================
# Part 1: FOXC1 vs PAM50 LumA-ness (pLumA) — the "by definition" lineage axis
# ============================================================
pam50_pred <- intrinsic.cluster.predict(
  sbt.model = pam50.robust, data = lumA_expr, annot = annot, do.mapping = TRUE)
print(colnames(pam50_pred$cor))            # confirm a "LumA" column exists
## [1] "Basal"  "Her2"   "LumA"   "LumB"   "Normal"
pLumA <- pam50_pred$cor[, "LumA"]
cat("FOXC1 vs pLumA: rho =",
    round(cor(foxc1, pLumA, method = "spearman"), 3),
    "(negative expected by construction)\n")
## FOXC1 vs pLumA: rho = 0.005 (negative expected by construction)
# ============================================================
# Part 2: FOXC1 vs purity in the CANONICAL radiation-naive cohort (surv_norad)
# ============================================================
# [REVIEW FIX] This chunk previously re-derived the radiation-naive cohort from brca.clin,
# producing a DIFFERENT n (182/24) vs the main pipeline's surv_norad (218/21). Now reuses
# surv_norad so the entire file reports one consistent cohort.
nr <- surv_norad
nr$centaur <- factor(ifelse(nr$group == "Centaur", "Centaur", "Non_centaur"),
                     levels = c("Non_centaur", "Centaur"))

# Align purity to surv_norad barcodes
.lumA_bc_all <- sample_meta_tumor$barcode[lumA_idx]
names(purity) <- .lumA_bc_all
nr$purity <- as.numeric(purity[nr$barcode])

cat("No-radiation cohort: n =", nrow(nr), ", events =", sum(nr$event), "\n")
## No-radiation cohort: n = 218 , events = 21
# ---- FOXC1 vs purity (admixture check) ----
if (any(!is.na(nr$purity))) {
  cat("FOXC1 vs tumor purity: rho =",
      round(cor(nr$foxc1, nr$purity, method = "spearman",
                use = "complete.obs"), 3), "\n")
} else {
  cat("FOXC1 vs tumor purity: skipped (no real purity vector available).\n")
}
## FOXC1 vs tumor purity: rho = -0.486
# ============================================================
# Part 3: purity-adjusted Cox in the canonical radiation-naive cohort
# ============================================================

if (any(!is.na(nr$purity))) {
  cox_p  <- coxph(Surv(time, event) ~ purity,           data = nr)
  cox_cp <- coxph(Surv(time, event) ~ centaur + purity, data = nr)
  cat("\n[REAL purity] alone HR:     ", round(exp(coef(cox_p)), 3), "\n")
  cat("[REAL purity] Centaur | purity HR: ",
      round(exp(coef(cox_cp))["centaurCentaur"], 3),
      "(does centaur add info beyond TRUE purity?)\n")
  print(summary(cox_cp)$coefficients)
} else {
  cat("\n[purity-adjusted Cox skipped] No real purity vector supplied.\n",
      " The luminal-basal 'lineage_contrast' is NOT a purity estimate and is not used here.\n")
}
## 
## [REAL purity] alone HR:      19.168 
## [REAL purity] Centaur | purity HR:  0.312 (does centaur add info beyond TRUE purity?)
##                      coef exp(coef)  se(coef)          z   Pr(>|z|)
## centaurCentaur -1.1654475 0.3117831 0.6164674 -1.8905258 0.05868768
## purity          0.9034307 2.4680558 2.1877358  0.4129524 0.67964149

10 External Validation (scRNA-seq GSE176078)

sf <- "data/GSE176078_seurat.rds"
scrna_ok <- file.exists(sf)
if (scrna_ok) { library(Seurat); seu <- readRDS(sf)
  ct_col <- "celltype_subset" }

10.1 Normal Tissue: FOXC1 Marks Luminal Progenitors

if (scrna_ok) {
  nl <- seu@meta.data[[ct_col]] %in% c("Luminal Progenitors","Mature Luminal")
  sn <- subset(seu, cells=colnames(seu)[nl])
  f1 <- FetchData(sn, vars="FOXC1")$FOXC1; g <- sn@meta.data[[ct_col]]
  lp_pct <- 100*mean(f1[g=="Luminal Progenitors"]>0)
  ml_pct <- 100*mean(f1[g=="Mature Luminal"]>0)
  pt <- prop.test(c(sum(f1[g=="Luminal Progenitors"]>0), sum(f1[g=="Mature Luminal"]>0)),
                   c(sum(g=="Luminal Progenitors"), sum(g=="Mature Luminal")))
  cat(sprintf("FOXC1+ in Luminal Progenitors: %.1f%%\n", lp_pct))
  cat(sprintf("FOXC1+ in Mature Luminal: %.1f%%\n", ml_pct))
  cat(sprintf("Fold enrichment: %.1fx, prop.test p = %.2e\n", lp_pct/ml_pct, pt$p.value))
} else cat("scRNA not loaded.\n")
## FOXC1+ in Luminal Progenitors: 21.9%
## FOXC1+ in Mature Luminal: 5.8%
## Fold enrichment: 3.8x, prop.test p = 1.25e-32

10.2 Hybrid vs Mosaic (Cancer LumA SC)

if (scrna_ok) {
  sl <- subset(seu, cells=colnames(seu)[seu@meta.data[[ct_col]]=="Cancer LumA SC"])

  # Hybrid test 1: FOXC1-ESR1 co-detection enrichment
  cd <- FetchData(sl, vars=c("FOXC1","ESR1"))>0
  ft <- fisher.test(table(cd[,"FOXC1"], cd[,"ESR1"]))
  cat("FOXC1-ESR1 co-detection (hybrid test):\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Fisher OR = %.2f, p = %.2e (OR>1 = HYBRID, not admixture)\n",
      ft$estimate, ft$p.value))

  # === [REVIEW FIX] Depth-adjusted co-detection ============================
  # Shared per-cell library size co-drops BOTH genes (low-depth cells read 0/0, high-depth read
  # 1/1), which INFLATES a raw Fisher OR -- it does NOT bias it toward the null. So the manuscript's
  # "dropout biases toward null / conservative" sentence is the wrong direction. Control depth:
  .codet <- data.frame(
    ESR1_pos  = as.integer(cd[,"ESR1"]),
    FOXC1_pos = as.integer(cd[,"FOXC1"]),
    log_umi   = log1p(sl$nCount_RNA),    # sequencing depth
    log_gene  = log1p(sl$nFeature_RNA))  # genes detected (detection capacity)
  .m_adj <- glm(ESR1_pos ~ FOXC1_pos + log_umi + log_gene, data=.codet, family=binomial)
  cat(sprintf("  Depth-adjusted co-detection: OR = %.2f, p = %.2e (logistic, + log-UMI + log-#genes)\n",
      exp(coef(.m_adj)["FOXC1_pos"]), summary(.m_adj)$coefficients["FOXC1_pos","Pr(>|z|)"]))
  cat("  -> OR stays >1, p<0.05: depth-robust; rewrite the 'conservative/toward-null' sentence.\n")
  cat("  -> OR collapses to ~1: drop the Fisher OR; rely on within-patient rho + luminal retention.\n")
  # (If the assay is not named 'RNA', swap nCount_RNA / nFeature_RNA for the correct slot.)

  # Hybrid test 2: module scores, within-cell and within-patient correlation
  sl <- AddModuleScore(sl, features=list(c("ESR1","FOXA1","GATA3","XBP1","AGR2")), name="lum")
  sl <- AddModuleScore(sl, features=list(c("KRT5","KRT14","KRT17","FOXC1","EGFR")), name="bas")
  md <- sl@meta.data
  cat(sprintf("\nPooled luminal-basal score correlation: %.3f\n",
      cor(md$lum1, md$bas1, method="spearman")))
  pp <- md %>% group_by(orig.ident) %>% filter(n()>=30) %>%
    summarise(r = cor(lum1, bas1, method="spearman"), .groups="drop")
  cat(sprintf("Median WITHIN-patient correlation: %.3f (~0 = not mosaic)\n",
      median(pp$r, na.rm=TRUE)))

  # Hybrid test 2b: FOXC1+ cells RETAIN luminal identity (no loss of luminal program)
  fp <- FetchData(sl, vars="FOXC1")$FOXC1 > 0
  wl <- wilcox.test(md$lum1[fp], md$lum1[!fp])
  cat(sprintf("\nLuminal score, FOXC1+ vs FOXC1- cancer cells: %.3f vs %.3f (Wilcoxon p = %.2f)\n",
      mean(md$lum1[fp]), mean(md$lum1[!fp]), wl$p.value))
  cat("  -> FOXC1+ cells retain luminal identity (no significant loss)\n")

  # Hybrid test 3: basal score unimodality
  if (has_diptest) {
    dp <- diptest::dip.test(md$bas1)
    cat(sprintf("Basal score dip test p = %.3f (p>0.05 = unimodal = gradient)\n", dp$p.value))
  }

  # Hybrid test 4: identity retention by Wu's OWN SCSubtype classification
  #   Uses Wu's defined cell identities (SCSubtype calls), not hand-picked markers.
  #   Among cancer cells from the same ER+ tumors, do FOXC1+ cells stay Luminal-SC
  #   or flip to Basal-SC? Retained Luminal-SC = identity preserved per Wu.
  luma_pts   <- unique(sl@meta.data$orig.ident)                  # ER+ tumors (had LumA SC cells)
  cancer_lab <- grep("^Cancer.*SC$", as.character(unique(seu@meta.data[[ct_col]])), value=TRUE)
  in_set <- seu@meta.data$orig.ident %in% luma_pts &
            seu@meta.data[[ct_col]] %in% cancer_lab
  cc  <- subset(seu, cells = colnames(seu)[in_set])
  ccd <- FetchData(cc, vars="FOXC1")$FOXC1 > 0
  is_basal <- grepl("Basal", cc@meta.data[[ct_col]])
  cat("\nWu SCSubtype identity (cancer cells from ER+ tumors):\n")
  cat(sprintf("  Basal-SC call among FOXC1+ cells: %.1f%%\n", 100*mean(is_basal[ccd])))
  cat(sprintf("  Basal-SC call among FOXC1- cells: %.1f%%\n", 100*mean(is_basal[!ccd])))
  cat("  -> FOXC1+ cancer cells largely keep Wu's Luminal SCSubtype call = retained identity\n")
  # Gene-level alternative: load Wu's SCSubtype signature genes from the
  # Swarbricklab-code/BrCa_cell_atlas GitHub repo and AddModuleScore with those sets.
}
## FOXC1-ESR1 co-detection (hybrid test):
##   Fisher OR = 3.99, p = 2.02e-04 (OR>1 = HYBRID, not admixture)
##   Depth-adjusted co-detection: OR = 3.46, p = 4.38e-03 (logistic, + log-UMI + log-#genes)
##   -> OR stays >1, p<0.05: depth-robust; rewrite the 'conservative/toward-null' sentence.
##   -> OR collapses to ~1: drop the Fisher OR; rely on within-patient rho + luminal retention.
## 
## Pooled luminal-basal score correlation: -0.137
## Median WITHIN-patient correlation: -0.031 (~0 = not mosaic)
## 
## Luminal score, FOXC1+ vs FOXC1- cancer cells: 0.359 vs 0.399 (Wilcoxon p = 0.79)
##   -> FOXC1+ cells retain luminal identity (no significant loss)
## Basal score dip test p = 0.000 (p>0.05 = unimodal = gradient)
## 
## Wu SCSubtype identity (cancer cells from ER+ tumors):
##   Basal-SC call among FOXC1+ cells: 46.8%
##   Basal-SC call among FOXC1- cells: 9.5%
##   -> FOXC1+ cancer cells largely keep Wu's Luminal SCSubtype call = retained identity

10.3 Progenitor Markers in FOXC1+ Cancer Cells (honest: trend, NS)

if (scrna_ok) {
  lp_mk <- c("KRT15","SLPI","ALDH1A3","PROM1","ELF5")
  lp_mk <- lp_mk[lp_mk %in% rownames(sl)]
  d <- FetchData(sl, vars=c("FOXC1", lp_mk)); d$fp <- d$FOXC1>0
  res <- data.frame()
  for (g in lp_mk) {
    wt <- wilcox.test(d[[g]][d$fp], d[[g]][!d$fp])
    res <- rbind(res, data.frame(marker=g,
      FOXC1pos=round(mean(d[[g]][d$fp]),4), FOXC1neg=round(mean(d[[g]][!d$fp]),4),
      ratio=round(mean(d[[g]][d$fp])/mean(d[[g]][!d$fp]),2), p=signif(wt$p.value,3)))
  }
  res$fdr <- signif(p.adjust(res$p, "BH"), 3)
  knitr::kable(res, caption="LP markers in FOXC1+ Cancer LumA cells (trend, mostly NS; BH-corrected)")
}
LP markers in FOXC1+ Cancer LumA cells (trend, mostly NS; BH-corrected)
marker FOXC1pos FOXC1neg ratio p fdr
KRT15 0.4833 0.3464 1.40 0.0767 0.192
SLPI 0.7552 0.5923 1.27 0.0597 0.192
ALDH1A3 0.0219 0.0135 1.62 0.1240 0.207
PROM1 0.0082 0.0042 1.94 0.2220 0.278
ELF5 0.0305 0.0323 0.94 0.6420 0.642

11 Summary

# ============================================================
#  Assemble multi-panel figures and export to figures_final/
#  Composites use patchwork; panels are auto-labelled A, B, C...
#  Edit the groupings below freely -- each `fig*` block is one
#  manuscript figure. pheatmap (heatmap) and survminer (KM)
#  are saved via a graphics device since they are not ggplots.
# ============================================================
library(patchwork)
dir.create("figures_final", showWarnings = FALSE)

# cairo_pdf embeds Unicode (rho) and fonts cleanly; fall back to pdf if absent
.dev <- if (capabilities("cairo")) cairo_pdf else grDevices::pdf
save_fig <- function(p, file, w, h)
  ggsave(file.path("figures_final", file), plot = p, width = w, height = h, device = .dev)

# ---- Figure 1: defining the phenotype (distribution + FOXCUT-FOXC1) ----
fig1 <- (p1 | p2) + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
save_fig(fig1, "fig1_phenotype_definition.pdf", 12, 5)

# ---- Figure 3: immune + stromal microenvironment (xCell2 panel if available) ----
if (exists("fig_xcell2") && !is.null(fig_xcell2)) {
  fig3 <- (fig_immune | fig_stromal) / fig_xcell2 + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
  save_fig(fig3, "fig3_immune_stromal.pdf", 14, 13)
} else {
  fig3 <- (fig_immune | fig_stromal) + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
  save_fig(fig3, "fig3_immune_stromal.pdf", 14, 8)
}

# ---- Figure 5: lineage quantification + proliferative quiescence ----
if (exists("fig_ssgsea") && !is.null(fig_ssgsea)) {
  fig5 <- fig_ssgsea / (fig_prolif_box | fig_prolif_cor) + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
  save_fig(fig5, "fig5_lineage_proliferation.pdf", 12, 11)
} else {
  fig5 <- (fig_prolif_box | fig_prolif_cor) + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
  save_fig(fig5, "fig5_lineage_proliferation.pdf", 10, 6)
}

# ---- Figure 6: mechanism -- progenitor / paracrine / inflammatory ----
fig6 <- fig_prog / fig_para / fig_inflam + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
save_fig(fig6, "fig6_mechanism.pdf", 10, 15)

# ---- Figure 7: independence -- age confound + orthogonality to risk tools ----
fig7 <- (fig_foxc1_age | fig_age_partial) / fig_ortho + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
save_fig(fig7, "fig7_independence.pdf", 12, 12)

# ---- Figure 4: dual-lineage heatmap (pheatmap gtable -> ggsave) ----
if (exists("ph_lineage")) {
  ggsave("figures_final/fig4_dual_lineage_heatmap.pdf", ph_lineage$gtable,
         width = 12, height = 8, device = .dev)
}

# ---- Figure 2: survival KM for FOXCUT and FOXC1, naive vs irradiated ----
# arrange_ggsurvplots() saves blank under ggplot2 >= 4.0 (S7); assemble $plot/$table via an
# explicit design grid (nesting + plot_layout locks dims and miscounts panels).
if (exists("km_foxcut_norad")) {
  km_foxcut_norad$plot <- km_foxcut_norad$plot + labs(tag = "A")
  km_foxc1_norad$plot  <- km_foxc1_norad$plot  + labs(tag = "B")
  km_foxcut_rad$plot   <- km_foxcut_rad$plot   + labs(tag = "C")
  km_foxc1_rad$plot    <- km_foxc1_rad$plot    + labs(tag = "D")
  km_design <- "AB\nCD\nEF\nGH"   # rows: norad curves / norad tables / rad curves / rad tables
  km_fig <-
    km_foxcut_norad$plot  + km_foxc1_norad$plot +
    km_foxcut_norad$table + km_foxc1_norad$table +
    km_foxcut_rad$plot    + km_foxc1_rad$plot +
    km_foxcut_rad$table   + km_foxc1_rad$table +
    plot_layout(design = km_design, heights = c(3, 1, 3, 1))
  ggsave("figures_final/fig2_survival_km.pdf", km_fig, width = 14, height = 13, device = .dev)
}

cat("Saved to figures_final/:\n"); print(list.files("figures_final"))
## Saved to figures_final/:
## [1] "fig1_phenotype_definition.pdf"  "fig2_survival_km.pdf"          
## [3] "fig3_immune_stromal.pdf"        "fig4_dual_lineage_heatmap.pdf" 
## [5] "fig5_lineage_proliferation.pdf" "fig6_mechanism.pdf"            
## [7] "fig7_independence.pdf"
## CENTAUR STATE — VALIDATED FINDINGS
## ==================================
## S1: FOXC1 normal distribution in LumA; median split
## S2: Survival HR~0.28 (no-rad); censor-robust; PH holds
## S3-5: Immune/stromal/xCell2 correlations
## S6: Dual lineage; proliferative quiescence; progenitor; paracrine by source; 3-tier inflammatory
## S7: Progenitor mediates ~67%; cytotoxic dominant; age=suppressor
## S8: Orthogonal to Oncotype/MammaPrint/ROR; cuts across Poudel
## S9: FOXC1 marks normal progenitors (3.8x); hybrid>mosaic; cancer-cell progenitor trend NS

Generated 2026-06-13 20:47:22.698272 | Median split log2=8.6 | No-rad n=218, 21 events

12 Review Additions — optional mediation & validation scope

12.1 MHC class I and xCell2-CD8 mediation (the two adjustments named in the abstract)

# [ADDED, runs] The two extra mediation adjustments named in the abstract: an MHC class I
# antigen-presentation score and an xCell2-derived CD8+ T-cell score, in the same age+stage
# adjusted style as s7_chain. CAVEAT (unchanged and important): adding a covariate collinear
# with the Centaur axis attenuates the HR mechanically -- this is "consistent with", NOT proof
# of mediation or a "causal chain / primary mechanism". Report it that way.
med_rows <- list()

# MHC class I antigen-presentation score
surv_cc$mhc1_z <- make_score(c("HLA-A","HLA-B","HLA-C","B2M","TAP1","TAP2"), surv_cc$barcode)
med_rows[["+ MHC class I"]] <- extract_hr(
  coxph(Surv(time,event) ~ group+age+stage_clean+mhc1_z, data=surv_cc))

# xCell2-derived CD8+ T-cell score (active CD8, matching paper Section J)
xf <- "results/rds/xcell2_scores.rds"
if (file.exists(xf)) {
  xc <- readRDS(xf)
  if (any(grepl("^TCGA", rownames(xc))) && !any(grepl("^TCGA", colnames(xc)))) xc <- t(xc)
  cd8_all <- grep("CD8", rownames(xc), value=TRUE, ignore.case=TRUE)
  cd8_row <- cd8_all[grepl("CD8-positive", cd8_all, ignore.case=TRUE) &
                     !grepl("naive|memory|exhaust", cd8_all, ignore.case=TRUE)][1]
  if (is.na(cd8_row) && length(cd8_all)) cd8_row <- cd8_all[1]
  if (!is.na(cd8_row)) {
    nb <- function(x) toupper(gsub("\\.", "-", x))
    xb <- nb(colnames(xc)); sb <- nb(surv_cc$barcode); k <- NA
    for (kk in c(28,19,16,15,12)) if (length(intersect(substr(sb,1,kk), substr(xb,1,kk)))>0) { k <- kk; break }
    if (!is.na(k)) {
      sc <- as.numeric(xc[cd8_row, ]); names(sc) <- substr(xb,1,k); sc <- sc[!duplicated(names(sc))]
      surv_cc$cd8x_z <- scale(sc[substr(sb,1,k)])[,1]
      sc8 <- surv_cc[!is.na(surv_cc$cd8x_z), ]
      med_rows[["+ xCell2 CD8"]] <- extract_hr(
        coxph(Surv(time,event) ~ group+age+stage_clean+cd8x_z, data=sc8))
    }
  }
}

out <- do.call(rbind, c(
  list(cbind(Model="age+stage baseline",
             extract_hr(coxph(Surv(time,event)~group+age+stage_clean, data=surv_cc)))),
  lapply(names(med_rows), function(n) cbind(Model=n, med_rows[[n]]))))
knitr::kable(out, row.names=FALSE,
  caption="Abstract-named mediators (MHC class I, xCell2 CD8); HR attenuation is collinearity, not proof of mediation")
Abstract-named mediators (MHC class I, xCell2 CD8); HR attenuation is collinearity, not proof of mediation
Model HR Lower Upper p
age+stage baseline 0.422 0.132 1.345 0.145
+ MHC class I 0.495 0.153 1.603 0.241
+ xCell2 CD8 0.537 0.165 1.748 0.301 
if (is.null(surv_cc$cd8x_z)) cat("xCell2 CD8 step skipped (scores file or CD8 row not found).\n")

13 Review Additions — paper analyses consolidated from the other pipelines

These blocks were present in the figure/mechanism pipelines and correspond to claims made in the manuscript, but were not in the master. Each is adapted to this file’s objects (norm_counts, lumA_idx, foxc1_lumA, get_ids/lookup_id, the imm table, the new GSVA ssgseaParam API) and guarded so the knit degrades gracefully. NOT knit-tested against data.

13.1 [backs Section J] xCell2 score provenance (documentation only)

# How results/rds/xcell2_scores.rds (loaded in s5_xcell2) was generated. eval=FALSE: this is a
# record of method, not re-run here. Requires the TPM tumor matrix + the xCell2 package.
gene_symbols <- mapIds(org.Hs.eg.db, keys = rownames(tpm_counts_tumor),
                       column = "SYMBOL", keytype = "ENSEMBL", multiVals = "first")
tpm_sym <- tpm_counts_tumor; rownames(tpm_sym) <- gene_symbols
tpm_sym <- tpm_sym[!is.na(rownames(tpm_sym)) & !duplicated(rownames(tpm_sym)), ]
data("PanCancer.xCell2Ref", package = "xCell2")          # BRCA-optimized ref if available
xcell2_scores <- xCell2::xCell2Analysis(mix = tpm_sym, xcell2object = PanCancer.xCell2Ref,
                                        spillover = TRUE, BPPARAM = BiocParallel::SerialParam())
saveRDS(xcell2_scores, "results/rds/xcell2_scores.rds")

13.2 [backs Section H.5] Chemokine–myeloid dissociation

# Recruitment chemokines track FOXC1, but the myeloid CELL markers that would receive them do not
# -> "a recruitment signal without the receiving population". Uses the Section 3 'imm' table.
if (exists("imm")) {
  diss_genes <- c("CCL19","CCL21","CXCL13","CXCL1","CCL2","CCL4","CXCL8",   # recruitment chemokines
                  "CD68","ITGAM","CD163","MRC1","CSF1R")                     # myeloid-cell markers
  diss <- imm[as.character(imm$gene) %in% diss_genes, c("gene","cat","rho","p","fdr")]
  diss$gene <- as.character(diss$gene)
  knitr::kable(diss[order(-diss$rho), ], row.names = FALSE,
    caption = "Chemokine-myeloid dissociation: recruitment chemokines (rho>0) vs myeloid-cell markers (rho~0)")
} else {
  cat("Run Section 3 first (the 'imm' table is built there).\n")
}
Chemokine-myeloid dissociation: recruitment chemokines (rho>0) vs myeloid-cell markers (rho~0)
gene cat rho p fdr
CCL21 TLS 0.557 0.00e+00 0.0000000
CCL19 TLS 0.517 0.00e+00 0.0000000
CXCL1 Myeloid 0.423 0.00e+00 0.0000000
CCL2 Myeloid 0.395 0.00e+00 0.0000000
MRC1 Myeloid 0.378 0.00e+00 0.0000000
CCL4 Other 0.344 0.00e+00 0.0000000
CXCL13 TLS 0.221 1.00e-07 0.0000001
CD163 Myeloid 0.150 3.11e-04 0.0003487
CD68 Myeloid 0.033 4.27e-01 0.4388611
ITGAM Myeloid -0.007 8.64e-01 0.8640000

13.3 [backs Section D/K: “not EMT — epithelial identity retained”] Epithelial vs EMT markers

# Expectation: epithelial markers are retained (not strongly negative with FOXC1) and EMT markers
# are NOT elevated -> the basal program is a lineage hybrid, not an epithelial-mesenchymal transition.
epi_markers <- c("EPCAM","CDH1","KRT18","KRT19","KRT8","MUC1")
emt_markers <- c("VIM","CDH2","SNAI1","SNAI2","TWIST1","ZEB1","ZEB2","FN1")
.emt_one <- function(sym, cat) {
  gid <- lookup_id(sym); if (is.na(gid)) return(NULL)
  ct <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(foxc1_lumA, as.numeric(norm_counts[gid, lumA_idx]),
                                  method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
  data.frame(gene = sym, category = cat, rho = round(ct$estimate, 3), p = signif(ct$p.value, 3))
}
emt_res <- do.call(rbind, c(lapply(epi_markers, .emt_one, cat = "Epithelial"),
                            lapply(emt_markers, .emt_one, cat = "EMT")))
emt_res$fdr <- signif(p.adjust(emt_res$p, "BH"), 3)
knitr::kable(emt_res[order(emt_res$category, -emt_res$rho), ], row.names = FALSE,
  caption = "FOXC1 vs epithelial / EMT markers in LumA (BH-corrected) -- tests the 'not EMT' claim")
FOXC1 vs epithelial / EMT markers in LumA (BH-corrected) – tests the ‘not EMT’ claim
gene category rho p fdr
SNAI2 EMT 0.483 0.00e+00 0.000000
VIM EMT 0.480 0.00e+00 0.000000
TWIST1 EMT 0.466 0.00e+00 0.000000
ZEB2 EMT 0.462 0.00e+00 0.000000
SNAI1 EMT 0.434 0.00e+00 0.000000
ZEB1 EMT 0.408 0.00e+00 0.000000
CDH2 EMT 0.025 5.50e-01 0.642000
FN1 EMT 0.022 6.05e-01 0.652000
MUC1 Epithelial -0.003 9.46e-01 0.946000
KRT8 Epithelial -0.060 1.53e-01 0.195000
EPCAM Epithelial -0.108 9.76e-03 0.013700
KRT18 Epithelial -0.132 1.53e-03 0.002380
CDH1 Epithelial -0.159 1.40e-04 0.000245
KRT19 Epithelial -0.166 7.06e-05 0.000141

13.4 [backs Limitations & Section K.2: compositional / CAF confound] CAF-adjusted partial correlation

# Does adjusting for CAF/FRC content kill the FOXC1 -> chemokine correlation?
#   drop_pct < 20%  -> tumor-autonomous; 20-50% -> partially CAF-mediated; >50% -> largely CAF-mediated.
# This is the confound a reviewer will specifically demand. (From chemokine_mechanism_alternatives.)
caf_sigs <- list(
  CAF_general = c("FAP","ACTA2","PDGFRA","PDGFRB","S100A4","COL1A1","COL1A2","COL3A1","DCN","LUM"),
  FRC_like    = c("PDPN","CCL19","CCL21","IL33","CXCL12","CD34","BMP4","ICAM1","VCAM1"),
  iCAF        = c("IL6","CXCL12","CXCL1","CXCL2","HAS1","PDGFRA","DPT"),
  myCAF       = c("ACTA2","MYL9","TAGLN","TPM1","TPM2","MMP11","POSTN"),
  HEV         = c("PLVAP","GLYCAM1","CCL21","CHST4","MADCAM1","SELE","SELP","ICAM1"))
caf_sig_ids <- lapply(caf_sigs, get_ids)
caf_f <- "results/rds/stromal_signatures.rds"
if (file.exists(caf_f)) {
  caf_ss <- readRDS(caf_f)
} else {
  par_caf <- ssgseaParam(exprData = as.matrix(log2(norm_counts + 1)), geneSets = caf_sig_ids)
  caf_ss  <- gsva(par_caf, verbose = FALSE); saveRDS(caf_ss, caf_f)
}
caf_cols <- match(sample_meta_tumor$barcode[lumA_idx], colnames(caf_ss))
ok <- !is.na(caf_cols); fx_ok <- foxc1_lumA[ok]; caf_ok <- caf_cols[ok]

sig_cor <- do.call(rbind, lapply(rownames(caf_ss), function(s) {
  ct <- cor.test(fx_ok, as.numeric(caf_ss[s, caf_ok]), method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  data.frame(signature = s, rho = round(ct$estimate, 3), p = signif(ct$p.value, 3)) }))
knitr::kable(sig_cor, row.names = FALSE, caption = "FOXC1 vs stromal/CAF ssGSEA signatures in LumA")
FOXC1 vs stromal/CAF ssGSEA signatures in LumA
signature rho p
CAF_general 0.435 0
FRC_like 0.672 0
iCAF 0.672 0
myCAF 0.356 0
HEV 0.611 0 
if (requireNamespace("ppcor", quietly = TRUE)) {
  .pcor_one <- function(chemok, ctrl_sig) {
    gid <- lookup_id(chemok); if (is.na(gid)) return(NULL)
    tv <- as.numeric(norm_counts[gid, lumA_idx])[ok]; cv <- as.numeric(caf_ss[ctrl_sig, caf_ok])
    d  <- data.frame(fc1 = fx_ok, target = tv, ctrl = cv); d <- d[complete.cases(d), ]
    raw <- cor(d$fc1, d$target, method = "spearman")
    pc  <- ppcor::pcor.test(d$fc1, d$target, d$ctrl, method = "spearman")
    data.frame(chemokine = chemok, control = ctrl_sig, raw_rho = round(raw, 3),
               partial_rho = round(pc$estimate, 3), partial_p = signif(pc$p.value, 3),
               drop_pct = round(100 * (abs(raw) - abs(pc$estimate)) / abs(raw), 1)) }
  pcr <- do.call(rbind, list(
    .pcor_one("CCL19","FRC_like"),    .pcor_one("CCL21","FRC_like"),
    .pcor_one("CCL5","FRC_like"),     .pcor_one("CXCL9","FRC_like"),
    .pcor_one("CCL19","CAF_general"), .pcor_one("CCL21","CAF_general"),
    .pcor_one("CCL5","CAF_general"),  .pcor_one("CCL21","HEV")))
  knitr::kable(pcr, row.names = FALSE,
    caption = "Partial correlation: FOXC1-chemokine controlling for stromal signature (drop_pct = % attenuation)")
} else {
  cat("Install 'ppcor' to run the CAF-confound partial-correlation test.\n")
}
Partial correlation: FOXC1-chemokine controlling for stromal signature (drop_pct = % attenuation)
chemokine control raw_rho partial_rho partial_p drop_pct
CCL19 FRC_like 0.517 -0.106 1.15e-02 79.5
CCL21 FRC_like 0.557 0.021 6.20e-01 96.3
CCL5 FRC_like 0.408 -0.152 2.61e-04 62.7
CXCL9 FRC_like 0.312 -0.186 7.70e-06 40.4
CCL19 CAF_general 0.517 0.467 0.00e+00 9.6
CCL21 CAF_general 0.557 0.500 0.00e+00 10.3
CCL5 CAF_general 0.408 0.328 0.00e+00 19.8
CCL21 HEV 0.557 0.111 7.91e-03 80.0

13.5 Exploratory mechanism tests — NOT in the manuscript (retained for completeness)

These test mechanisms the paper does not claim (FOXC1-direct transcription; hypoxia upstream). Kept eval=FALSE and demarcated so they are not mistaken for reported results.

# Hypoxia (Buffa 2010) as an alternative upstream driver of the chemokine signal.
buffa <- c("VEGFA","SLC2A1","CA9","LDHA","PGK1","HK2","ALDOA","ENO1","NDRG1","BNIP3",
           "P4HA1","ADM","MIF","TPI1","PGAM1")
par_hyp <- ssgseaParam(exprData = as.matrix(log2(norm_counts + 1)),
                       geneSets = list(hypoxia = get_ids(buffa)))
hyp_ss  <- gsva(par_hyp, verbose = FALSE)
hcols   <- match(sample_meta_tumor$barcode[lumA_idx], colnames(hyp_ss))
hok     <- !is.na(hcols)
if (requireNamespace("ppcor", quietly = TRUE)) {
  d <- data.frame(fc1 = foxc1_lumA[hok],
                  ccl19 = as.numeric(norm_counts[lookup_id("CCL19"), lumA_idx])[hok],
                  hyp   = as.numeric(hyp_ss["hypoxia", hcols[hok]]))
  d <- d[complete.cases(d), ]
  print(ppcor::pcor.test(d$fc1, d$ccl19, d$hyp, method = "spearman"))  # does hypoxia explain it?
}
# (1) FOXC1 motif enrichment in chemokine promoters (JASPAR PWM + Biostrings/genome) and
# (2) single-cell FOXC1/CCL19 co-expression by cell type, are in chemokine_mechanism_alternatives.Rmd.
# They need heavy external dependencies (JASPAR2020/TFBSTools/BSgenome; or a loaded Seurat sc_obj)
# that are out of scope for this consolidated file. See that file's Tests 3-4 for the full code.
